title: Advocacy platform
abstract: An advocacy system in which user devices each have an advocacy software application installed thereon. Each user device has a local data store accessible to the advocacy software application comprising records of asset demands logged by the user devices. Each user has associated therewith a predetermined list of user contacts registered with the advocacy system and the asset demand logged by an initiating user is shareable with said predetermined list of user contacts. The predetermined list of user contacts includes primary user contacts and each primary user contact has a further predetermined list of user contacts defining a further level of user contacts in a user contact hierarchy. The system uses a hierarchy threshold level parameter and advocacy responses to the asset demand are permitted by each advocacy software application according to the level of a responding user contact in the user contact hierarchy. The advocacy system may be used to manage referral of various assets, products and services.
claims:
Claims CLAIMS: 1 . An advocacy system comprising: user devices arranged for communication therebetween, each user device comprising an advocacy software application installed thereon, wherein each user device has a local data store accessible to the advocacy software application comprising records of asset demands logged by the user devices, each user having associated therewith a predetermined list of user contacts registered with the advocacy system and the asset demand logged by an initiating user being shareable with said predetermined list of user contacts said predetermined list of user contacts comprising primary user contacts and wherein each primary user contact has a further predetermined list of user contacts defining a further level of user contacts in a user contact hierarchy wherein the system has a hierarchy threshold level parameter and advocacy responses to the asset demand are permitted by each advocacy software application according to the level of a responding user contact in the user contact hierarchy. 2. An advocacy system according to claim 1 , wherein the advocacy application logs an acceptance or rejection of the advocacy responses in the local data store along with an identifier of the responding user contact. 3. An advocacy system according to claim 1 or 2, wherein the advocacy application logs actions taken by the initiating user and/or the responding user contact leading to acceptance or rejection of the advocacy response by the initiating user. 4. An advocacy system according to any preceding claim, wherein the hierarchy threshold parameter is set to two or three levels. 5. An advocacy system according to any preceding claim, wherein the advocacy application inhibits sharing of the asset demand beyond the hierarchy threshold level. 6. An advocacy system according to any preceding claim, wherein upon communication of an advocacy response by a user contact in the further level or a tertiary level in the user contact hierarchy, the advocacy application creates a log of the response including a record of any intermediate user contact in the one or more higher level of the user contact hierarchy. 7. An advocacy system according to any preceding claim, wherein the advocacy application permits an advocacy response by a user contact at one level in the user contact hierarchy based upon an approval by a user contact at the next higher level in the hierarchy. 8. An advocacy system according to any preceding claim, wherein a central server system monitors actions by user contacts upon receiving, or in response to, asset demands and determines a score for each user contact based on said actions. 9. An advocacy system according to claim 8, wherein the central server system monitors a number of advocacy responses by a user contact to each received asset demand and/or a ratio acceptance by the initiating user of advocacy responses by the user contact and determines a score for each user contact based upon said number or ratio. 10. An advocacy system according to claim 8 or 9, wherein access to the advocacy system by the user contact is controlled automatically based upon said score. 1 1 . An advocacy system according to claim 10, wherein access to individual functions of the advocacy system by the user contact is automatically permitted or denied based upon said score. 12. An advocacy system according to any preceding claim, wherein each user device comprises a local contact data store and the software application permits sharing of the asset demand only with user contacts listed in the local contact data store. 13. An advocacy system according to claim 12, wherein the software application restricts entry of user contact data into the local contact data store on the user device to contact data from one or more contact management application or contact storage location on the user device. 14. An advocacy system according to any preceding claim, wherein the local data store of each user device comprises an event table for use by the software application in synchronising data with the local data store of one or more further device, wherein a user interaction with the software application that alters the status of an asset demand or 

 advocacy response is recorded in the event table with a time of status change until it has been communicated with each of the one or more further device in the user contact hierarchy associated with the status alteration. 15. An advocacy system according to claim 14, wherein each entry in the event table comprises a unique identifier, the data/status change and an event type and time stamp. 16. An advocacy system according to claim 14 or 15, wherein upon creation of a new entry in the event table, the software application generates a push synchronisation signal from the user device to each of said one or more further device in the user contact hierarchy. 17. An advocacy system according to any one of claims 14 to 16,wherein upon receipt of a communication indicating a status change from a user device, the software application checks the time of the status change recorded by the user device against the time of a status change recorded for one or more preceding status change or in a preceding communication of a status change received from another user device and records the status change in the data store only if the times of the status changes and/or content of the status changes does not conflict. 18. An advocacy system according to any one of claims 14 to 17, wherein upon performing a data/status change on the local data store of the handset, the software application sends a synchronisation signal to the devices of the predetermined list(s) of user contacts within the threshold level of the user contact hierarchy. 19. An advocacy system according to any preceding claim, wherein user actions using the software application that affect the status of an asset demand or advocacy response are time stamped by the software application and communicated to the predetermined list(s) of user contacts within the threshold level of the user contact hierarchy with a time stamp. 20. An advocacy system according to any preceding claim, wherein the initiating user logs an intended period of asset use with the asset demand and the local data stores record an actual period of use of an asset adopted using the advocacy system over a period of time following fulfilment of the asset demand. 21 . An advocacy system according to claim 20, wherein the software application compares the actual period of use of the asset with the intended period of use for said asset and applies a success rating or score to the asset or a user contact advocating said asset in dependence thereon. 22. An advocacy system according to claim 20 or 21 , wherein the advocacy application applies a score to all user contacts in the different levels of the hierarchy associated with the advocacy response to the asset demand. 23. An advocacy system according to any preceding claim, wherein the advocacy application requests or receives a score or rating for an asset after adoption of the asset corresponding to the advocacy response. 24. An advocacy system according to any one of claims 8-1 1 or 19-23, wherein the advocacy application monitors one or more parameter indicative of success or failure of advocacy responses to asset demands and applies one or more machine learning routine, the output of which is used to identify automatically assets and/or user contacts for further asset demands. 25. An advocacy system comprising a platform arranged to communicate with user devices having an advocacy software application installed thereon, wherein each handset has a local data store comprising records of asset demands logged by the user devices, each user having associated therewith a predetermined list of user contacts registered with the advocacy system, the advocacy application limiting sharing of the asset demand logged by an initiating user with said predetermined list of user contacts, wherein the central server system monitors actions by said user contacts using the advocacy application in response to asset demands and determines a score for each user contact based on said actions, the central server system automatically controlling access to functionality the advocacy system by the user contacts based upon said score. 26. An advocacy system comprising a decentralised communication platform arranged for communication between user devices having an advocacy software application installed thereon, wherein the user handsets have local data stores comprising records of asset demands logged by the user devices, each user having associated therewith a predetermined list of user contacts registered with the advocacy system, the advocacy 

 application limiting sharing of the asset demand logged by an initiating user with said predetermined list of user contacts, wherein the advocacy software application on each user device logs a timestamp of an action taken by said user contact using the software application in response to an asset demand and sends a synchronisation signal to other handsets comprising said timestamp, the advocacy software application performing a conflict check based on said timestamp prior to recording the action in the local data store. 27. A data carrier comprising machine readable instructions for installation on a user device of an advocacy system, wherein the machine readable instructions comprise an advocacy software application when executed arranged to: access a local data store of the user device comprising records of asset demands logged by one or more the user device, access a predetermined list of user contacts registered with the advocacy system and the asset demand logged by an initiating user being shareable with said predetermined list of user contacts, said predetermined list of user contacts comprising primary user contacts and wherein each primary user contact has a further predetermined list of user contacts defining a further level of user contacts in a user contact hierarchy; identify a hierarchy threshold level parameter for each asset demand; and permit logging of advocacy responses to an asset demand according to a level of a responding user contact in the user contact hierarchy being within the hierarchy level threshold. 28. A method of controlling an advocacy system comprising: operating advocacy software application installed on multiple end user devices to permit communication between said user devices in relation to a common asset demand, maintaining a data store on each user device comprising records of asset demands logged by the user devices, maintaining a list of contacts registered with the advocacy system for each user, and limiting the sharing of each asset demand logged by an initiating user device with said list of contacts via the advocacy software application.
